hero.title: DevSecOps Engineer Salary Guide 2026
hero.description: As cloud security breaches and software supply-chain attacks rise, the demand for specialists who build secure infrastructure-as-code remains at an all-time high. Here is the comprehensive 2026 salary guide for DevSecOps Engineers.

experience.notes: Compensation is driven by hands-on scripting in Python/Bash, understanding CI/CD pipeline concepts, and basic cloud security certifications (AWS Security Specialist).
Requires autonomy in configuring SAST/DAST pipelines, managing HashiCorp Vault secrets, writing custom OPA policies, and hardening containerized workloads in Kubernetes.
Expected to architect multi-cloud security monitoring frameworks, lead SOC2/ISO audit evidence collection, automate VM threat modeling, and possess CKS credentials.
Involves defining corporate security automation governance, leading large platform security teams, managing third-party vendor risk, and receiving significant stock refreshers.

byStack.notes: Highly specialized engineering focusing on runtime kernel security and custom operator policies.
Standard enterprise stack requiring Checkov/tfsec integrations and IAM monitoring.
Traditional pipeline security focused on SAST/DAST scripting and token management.

skillsThatIncreaseSalary.reason: Expertise in securing running containers, deploying network policies, and debugging kernel escapes commands a major premium.
Companies transitioning to automated governance pay highly for engineers who write dry-run configuration audits.
Enables writing custom Kubernetes plugins, controllers, and highly efficient cloud-native security agents.

futureOutlook: DevSecOps Engineer salaries are projected to grow by 15% between 2026 and 2028. As regulatory oversight (like Europe's NIS2 and US Executive Orders) increases, companies are legally required to secure their pipelines. This shifts security from an IT cost center to a critical legal and commercial requirement, elevating DevSecOps engineers to core strategic positions. Furthermore, as organizations expand their cloud-native infrastructure, the need to secure Kubernetes and microservices will keep compensation high. You can compare infrastructure pay rates in our [Cloud Engineer Salary Guide 2026](/salary-guides/cloud-engineer-salary-guide-2026) for added context.
marketAnalysis: The market for DevSecOps Engineers in 2026 is experiencing a strong bifurcation. Companies have realized that simple 'out of the box' security scanners are not enough. They need specialized engineers who can integrate scanning without introducing pipeline latency or developer friction. There is a strong premium for candidates who understand systems engineering and software architecture. Hiring managers are looking for developers who understand security, rather than security administrators who cannot code. This hybrid requirement is why the talent pool is small, driving compensation packages higher. If you are drafting your application, check out our professional [DevSecOps Engineer Resume Examples](/resume-examples/devsecops-engineer) to ensure your experience matches this market demand.
careerPath: A career in DevSecOps provides a structured and lucrative path. Many professionals transition from junior software development or DevOps engineering. To understand how to become a security automation expert, read the [How to Become a DevSecOps Engineer Career Guide](/career-guides/how-to-become-devsecops-engineer). As you progress, you will move from executing static scans to architecting enterprise-wide policy structures. Senior engineers often face a choice: progress as a Principal Security Architect or transition to a management track as a DevSecOps Lead, eventually moving into roles like Director of Security Engineering or CISO. For a step-by-step layout of required skills at each phase, review the [DevSecOps Engineer Roadmap](/roadmaps/devsecops-engineer).
negotiationTips: Negotiating compensation as a DevSecOps Engineer requires highlighting your impact on deployment velocity and risk reduction. First, focus on developer hours saved by your automation. If you reduced security triage times by 50%, translate that into engineering dollars. Second, frame security as an enabler: automated compliance allows the company to pass audits (like SOC2) faster, securing sales contracts. Third, prepare for technical questions by reviewing the [DevSecOps Engineer Interview Questions](/interview-questions/devsecops-engineer) and [DevOps Interview Questions](/interview-questions/devops) guides. Showing a track record of running secure systems while maintaining a high deployment frequency is your strongest negotiating lever.
industryTrends: The security automation landscape is evolving rapidly, driving salary premiums for specialized skills. First, software supply-chain security is a top priority, requiring knowledge of SBOM generation and binary signature validation. Second, the adoption of GitOps requires integrating security checks inside configuration merge requests rather than live systems. Third, AI integration is changing daily work, with AI agents automatically proposing security fixes. Engineers who can audit and orchestrate these AI scanners command a premium. Finally, runtime observability using eBPF is growing, shifting the focus from static scanning to dynamic kernel monitoring.
certificationsAndSkills: To maximize your earning potential in 2026, focus on specialized credentials and high-income cloud skills. Core skills that drive salary include Kubernetes security (Gatekeeper, Calico), cloud networking security (VPC, IAM, KMS), and scripting (Python, Go, Bash). Highly respected certifications include the CKS (Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist) and AWS Certified Security - Specialty. Additionally, understanding compliance automation platforms (like Drata or Vanta) and their API integrations will make you a highly sought-after candidate for fast-growing SaaS startups.

faq.q: Why do DevSecOps Engineers earn more than standard DevOps Engineers?
faq.a: DevSecOps Engineers possess a hybrid skill set. They must understand infrastructure automation (DevOps) AND application security/compliance (Sec). This dual expertise is rare, leading to a smaller talent pool and higher salaries.

faq.q: What is the starting salary for an entry-level DevSecOps Engineer?
faq.a: Entry-level DevSecOps Engineers typically start between $90,000 and $115,000, depending on location, cloud certifications, and programming skills.

faq.q: Does location affect remote DevSecOps salaries?
faq.a: Yes. While remote work is common, many US companies anchor their remote salaries to local tech hub bands (e.g. San Francisco or New York), offering competitive geo-arbitrage opportunities.

faq.q: Will AI tools decrease DevSecOps salaries in the future?
faq.a: No. While AI tools automate simple code scanning and basic patching, human security engineers are required to audit AI outputs, write governance policies, and coordinate complex multi-system compliance.

faq.q: Which cloud provider security specialization pays the most?
faq.a: AWS remains the dominant enterprise cloud, followed closely by Azure in finance and government sectors. Specializing in AWS Security generally provides the highest volume of high-paying opportunities.

faq.q: What is the typical bonus structure for senior DevSecOps Engineers?
faq.a: Senior engineers usually receive annual performance bonuses of 10% to 20% of their base salary, along with stock options or annual RSU grants.

faq.q: Is a CISSP certification useful for increasing salary?
faq.a: Yes, especially for mid-to-senior levels looking to transition into architectural, governance, or security management roles.

faq.q: Should I learn Go or Python to increase my market value?
faq.a: Both are excellent. Python is the industry standard for writing pipeline automation. Go is highly valuable for building custom Kubernetes controllers and cloud-native security agents.

faq.q: How does compliance automation experience impact salary?
faq.a: Engineers who can automate SOC2 or ISO 27001 evidence collection command a 10% premium because they directly save audit preparation costs and accelerate sales cycles.

faq.q: Are there contract roles available for DevSecOps Engineers?
faq.a: Yes. Many companies hire contract DevSecOps consultants for 6-12 months to set up security pipelines or prepare for compliance audits, commanding hourly rates between $90 and $150+.
